---
title: "Working Papers"
---

*Drafts available on request.*

**Rosenberg, Andrew S.** "Scarcity, Worthiness, and the Politics of Refugee
Admission in the Global North." *R&R, Journal of Politics.*

> Examines why some refugee crises prompt generosity while others trigger
> restriction. Develops a formal model incorporating scarcity costs, refugee
> racial identity, and geopolitical salience, and tests predictions using
> country-year asylum policy data.

**Rosenberg, Andrew S.** "The Discretionary Color Line in International
Institutions."

> Tests whether formally equal rules eliminate racial hierarchy in practice,
> using the Schengen visa regime as a case. Ancestral distance predicts visa
> refusal rates, with busier consulates discriminating more.

Alarian, Hannah M., Michael Bernhard, and **Andrew S. Rosenberg**. "Traces of
the Past: Regime Histories and Anti-Foreigner Violence in Post-Unification
Germany."

> Investigates whether distinct regime histories explain disparate patterns of
> anti-foreigner violence in post-unification Germany, examining the impact of
> East–West "coming to terms with the past" approaches.

Mucci, Juliana K., Hannah M. Alarian, and **Andrew S. Rosenberg**. "Who
Deserves to Belong? Public Opinion and U.S. Naturalization Decisions."

**Rosenberg, Andrew S.** and Hannah M. Alarian. "National Pride and Prejudice:
How National Identity Moderates Anti-Immigrant Attitudes."

> Survey experiments testing whether connecting immigration to the NHS reduces
> anti-immigrant sentiment among strong national identifiers.

**Rosenberg, Andrew S.** "Immigration Does Not Lower Social Capital."

> Uses county-level data and an instrumental variable design to challenge the
> assumption that immigration diminishes social capital.

O'Dwyer, Conor, **Andrew S. Rosenberg**, and Matthew Stenberg. "Races at the
Bottom: Local Impacts of Official Discrimination on Election Outcomes."

> Analyzes Polish "LGBT Free Zones" and their impact on Law and Justice party
> election outcomes in 2024 municipal elections.

Alarian, Hannah M., Sebastian Elischer, and **Andrew S. Rosenberg**. "Voting
for Extremists: How Extremist Protests Influence Far-Right Voting Behavior in
Germany."

> Examines AfD proximity to right-wing extremist organizations and its
> electoral impact across election cycles 2013–2021.
